name: Intel CET — Control-flow Enforcement Technology
summary: Intel's hardware control-flow integrity: a hardware shadow stack (CALL pushes return addr; RET checks mismatch) plus Indirect Branch Tracking (indirect CALL/JMP must land on ENDBR opcode). Ships on Tiger Lake (11th gen, 2020) and later, AMD Zen 3+. Linux 6.4 (2023) added userspace shadow stack. Mainstream now.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Intel's **Control-flow Enforcement Technology** combines a hardware shadow stack (CALL pushes return addr; RET checks mismatch → #CP fault) and Indirect Branch Tracking (indirect CALL/JMP must land on `ENDBR` opcode). Shadow stack lives in special read-only-to-userspace pages; speculation also limited to ~2 instructions past non-ENDBR targets (Alder Lake+).

CET attacks the same exploit class as ARM PAC — backward- and forward-edge control-flow integrity — but uses a hardware-managed shadow stack rather than cryptographic pointer signing.

sections.title: Deployment
sections.content: Ships on Tiger Lake (11th gen, 2020) and later, AMD Zen 3+. Linux: CET-IBT in 5.18, **userspace shadow stack in 6.4 (2023)**. Windows 11 enables Hardware-enforced Stack Protection. Mainstream now.
